name: OpsLevel Internal Developer Portal API
what_it_does: Internal Developer Portal (IDP) with GraphQL and REST API — enabling platform engineering teams to catalog services, enforce engineering best practices via rubrics (scorecards), track team ownership, and provide service health insights, with integrations into GitHub, PagerDuty, Datadog, and other DevOps tools, built by former PagerDuty engineers.
use_cases: Cataloging microservices via OpsLevel API for service discovery and ownership tracking
Enforcing engineering rubrics via OpsLevel API for service quality compliance measurement
Tracking on-call ownership via OpsLevel API for incident routing and team accountability
Integrating tool data via OpsLevel API from GitHub, Datadog, Snyk for unified insights
Managing team structure via OpsLevel API for engineering organization mapping
not_for: Deployment automation (OpsLevel is catalog/standards — use Humanitec for deployment IDP)
Small teams without platform engineering function
Open-source self-hosted IDP (use Backstage for open-source)
best_when: An engineering organization needs service catalog with engineering rubrics/scorecards and team ownership tracking — built by ex-PagerDuty engineers with incident management DNA.
avoid_when: You need deployment automation, open-source, or are a small team without engineering standards programs.

auth.notes: API token via Authorization: Bearer header or graphql-token header. GraphQL endpoint: api.opslevel.com/graphql. REST API also available. Token from OpsLevel account settings. No OAuth2. Webhooks for service catalog events. GraphQL is the primary API interface. REST is secondary. Terraform provider available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — free trial available
pricing.notes: US company (San Francisco). Founded 2019 (Kyle Hanson, Jordan Frank — ex-PagerDuty). $15M+ funding. Built by ex-PagerDuty engineers — incident and reliability DNA. Rubrics: engineering best practices measurements (similar to Cortex scorecards). Service catalog: services, teams, lifecycle stages. Integrations: GitHub, GitLab, PagerDuty, Datadog, Snyk, Checkmarx, LaunchDarkly, Slack. GraphQL-first API. Terraform provider for GitOps-based service registration. YML-based service definitions. Competing with Cortex in engineering excellence IDP space.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL primary (not REST). Bearer token or graphql-token header. Webhooks. SOC2. Ex-PagerDuty founders. Terraform provider. No free tier. Per-user pricing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Service reads idempotent by ID. Rubric checks idempotent. Mutations: use GraphQL IDs for idempotent updates. Webhook events: implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL is primary API: use api.opslevel.com/graphql — REST API is secondary
graphql-token header (alternative to Authorization: Bearer) — both work
Terraform provider available for GitOps-based service registration (recommended for IaC teams)
Rubrics are the key differentiator vs service catalog tools — evaluate rubric configuration carefully
Per-user pricing ($20-30/month) — costs scale with team size
US data residency — verify for EU compliance requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Bearer token or custom graphql-token header. SOC2 certified. Service catalog data (ownership, rubric scores) is operationally sensitive. US data residency. Terraform provider for secure IaC-based registration. No OAuth2. GraphQL introspection should be disabled in production for security.
